errors.description: Attempted to create a Branch under an Organization belonging to a different tenant. Warn-level because a mismatch is a security signal (forged `organization_id` from a caller with cross-tenant visibility).
Same as above for Region. Same security-signal severity.
Attempted to create a Branch under an Organization with status='archived'. Response asks the caller to unarchive the org first OR pick another.
Attempted to change `organization_id` on an existing Branch. Immutable post-create — see readme §1.3. Response asks the caller to create a fresh Branch under the new org and close the old one.
Attempted to change `region_id`. Immutable — tax + currency + timezone implications. Same guidance as BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_IMMUTABLE.
Attempted to change `tenant_id`. Never permitted — tenancy isolation violation. Warn-level because a tenant_id write attempt from a non-platform-admin path is a security signal.
Composite unique (tenant_id, organization_id, slug) violated. Response includes a suggested variant slug.
Reached the `branch_slot` entitlement cap (Small=3, Medium=25, Enterprise=null). Response includes upgrade path + current slot usage count.
Attempted to unset is_default=true when no other Branch under the same (tenant, org) is default. Response asks the caller to promote another Branch first.
Attempted to close a Branch with active downstream references (teams, facilities, sessions, bookings, memberships). Response includes the dependent-row counts + a link to reassign them.
Attempted to reopen a closed Branch after a successor Branch was created under the same (tenant, organization) post-close. Response identifies the successor Branch's ULID + created_at.
opening_hours JSON failed the `valid_opening_hours` rule. Response identifies the offending day / period / field (e.g. `sunday[0].close before open`).
address JSON failed the `valid_address_structure` rule. Response identifies the missing required field (line1 / city / country_code).
coordinates JSON failed the `valid_coordinates` rule. Common causes: lat outside [-90, 90], lng outside [-180, 180], accuracy_m <= 0, unknown source value.
Attempted to submit opening_hours with > 1 period per day-key on a tenant lacking the `branch_multi_period_hours` entitlement. Response includes upgrade path.
Submitted amenity tag is not in the platform catalogue AND tenant lacks `branch_amenities: custom` (Enterprise). Response includes the offending tag(s) + a link to the catalogue reference.
Attempted `POST /branches/{branch}/geocode` on a tenant lacking the `branch_geocoding` entitlement. Response includes upgrade path.

Subprocessor returned an error or no result. Retryable — the job retries 3× with backoff before surfacing. `warn` severity because sustained failures often indicate the subprocessor API key rotated / quota exhausted.

Subprocessor returned a match with confidence < `branch.geocoding.quality_threshold`. Coordinates not persisted; falls back to `manual` mode. Common cause: address too vague (missing city or postal code).
Write attempted against a Branch with status=closed. Returned by `branch.resolve_active` middleware on booking-adjacent routes.
Write attempted against a Branch with status=paused. Returned by `branch.resolve_active` middleware on booking-adjacent routes; reads pass through.

Booking-adjacent write attempted outside the resolved open window (per branch.region.timezone + opening_hours + exceptions). Returned by `branch.enforce_operating_hours`. Response includes the next-open timestamp.
Attempted DELETE `/branches/{branch}`. Wave 2 has no public hard-delete — close is terminal. Response guides the caller to `POST /branches/{branch}/close`.
